description: walkValidate check inverted in GetInputVariable() — condition changed from '== walkValidate' to '!= walkValidate'. During the validate walk, actual variable values are now used instead of unknown values, causing validation to pass only for the specific input set rather than checking all possible values. During the plan walk, unknown values are returned instead of real ones, breaking planning. This affects context_plan.go which relies on walkValidate to pre-check configurations broadly.

Config nil check inverted in GetInputVariable() — condition changed from 'config == nil' to 'config != nil'. Declared variables are now treated as undeclared (returning DynamicVal with a spurious 'undeclared variable' error), while references to actually undeclared variables pass the check and proceed to NamedValues.GetInputVariableValue(), causing a panic when the key is not found.

hasUnknownKeys check inverted in GetResource() — condition changed from 'hasUnknownKeys' to '!hasUnknownKeys'. Resources with known for_each/count keys now incorrectly return DynamicVal (breaking all normal resource references in plan/apply), while resources with unknown keys bypass the early return and attempt full evaluation with unpredictable instance key types, causing panics or type errors.

Ephemeral check inverted in prepareFinalInputVariableValue() — condition changed from 'cfg.Ephemeral' to '!cfg.Ephemeral'. Non-ephemeral variables are now incorrectly marked as ephemeral (preventing them from being persisted to state or used in outputs), while truly ephemeral variables lose their mark (allowing ephemeral data to leak into state files and plan outputs). This interacts with evaluate.go's GetInputVariable() which also applies ephemeral marks based on config.

Apply-time variable validation removed from ApplyAndEval() — the checkApplyTimeVariables() call and its error check were deleted. Without this validation, apply proceeds even when required ephemeral variables are missing or have wrong types. This causes panics in evaluate.go's GetInputVariable() when it tries to read ephemeral variable values from NamedValues that were never populated.

NilHook.PreApply() returns HookActionHalt instead of HookActionContinue. Since NilHook is embedded by all custom hook implementations that only override specific methods, any hook that doesn't explicitly override PreApply will now return Halt. The hook dispatch in node_resource_apply_instance.go checks this return value and aborts the apply operation when any hook returns Halt, silently preventing all resource applications without producing an error diagnostic.
